name: cpig-kin-12-klann-walker
description: kin-12 Klann 6-bar Stephenson III walking linkage — Joe Klann's planar walking-leg patent (US 6,260,862). 5 moving bodies + 7 revolutes + 2 closed loops; planar Gruebler 1 DOF. Two ternary links: rocker (O4-B-D rigid) and leg (C-D-E rigid with E the foot trace). Crank rotates under 400 N·mm constant torque; the leg's foot E sweeps a boomerang coupler curve. Forward-dynamics integrates 2s; 5 Pose at Step taps (steps 0/500/1000/1500/2000) flatten into Pose Bound Bodies, rendering both leg and rocker bodies at all five poses simultaneously. Phase 4.6.F.4 multi-loop closed-loop solver demo. Pairs with kin_11 (single-loop 4-bar leg) as the operator's 'two walker variants'.

setup.vlmDescription: Joe Klann's 6-bar walking-leg linkage shown as a stop-motion strip of two bound bodies at five different crank angles. The LEG body (rendered as a thin closed triangle pipe through C-D-E, where E is the foot trace point) sweeps a boomerang/horseshoe-shaped trajectory below and forward of the chassis. The ROCKER body (rendered as a thin two-segment polyline pipe through O4-B-D) rocks about its fixed pivot O4 as the crank rotates. Both bodies are drawn at all five sampled poses (steps 0/500/1000/1500/2000 of a 2-second 400 N·mm constant-torque crank integration) so the viewer sees the leg's full 'walking' arc in one frame. A faint design-pose skeleton (fixed pivots O2 and O4, crank, two couplers) is drawn at 50% alpha behind via Preview Diagram in Display Mode 2.
